[  Running job: --help... 
Usage:
  [invocation] [options] [<targets>...]

Arguments:
  <targets>    A list of targets to run or list. If not specified, the "default" target will be run, or all targets will be listed.

Options:
  -c, --clear                Clear the console before execution
  -n, --dry-run              Do a dry run without executing actions
  -d, --list-dependencies    List all (or specified) targets and dependencies, then exit
  -i, --list-inputs          List all (or specified) targets and inputs, then exit
  -l, --list-targets         List all (or specified) targets, then exit
  -t, --list-tree            List all (or specified) targets and dependency trees, then exit
  -N, --no-color             Disable colored output
  -E, --no-extended-chars    Disable extended characters
  -p, --parallel             Run targets in parallel
  -s, --skip-dependencies    Do not run targets' dependencies
  -v, --verbose              Enable verbose output
  --appveyor                 Force Appveyor mode (normally auto-detected)
  --azure-pipelines          Force Azure Pipelines mode (normally auto-detected)
  --github-actions           Force GitHub Actions mode (normally auto-detected)
  --gitlab-ci                Force GitLab CI mode (normally auto-detected)
  --teamcity                 Force TeamCity mode (normally auto-detected)
  --travis                   Force Travis CI mode (normally auto-detected)
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information, then exit (case insensitive)

Remarks:
  The --list-xxx options may be combined.
  The invocation is typically a call to dotnet run, or the path to a script which wraps a call to dotnet run.

Examples:
  ./build.sh
  ./build.sh -d
  ./build.sh -t -i default
  ./build.sh test pack
  dotnet run --project targets -- -n build

Targets:
  build         
  c-token       
  synthesize    
  vaporize      
  validate
